fault_hypothesis: On line 65, when the counter exceeds the cap, it is set to `cap - 1` instead of `cap`, causing the counter to be pinned one below the cap value rather than at the cap.

constraints.intent: After capping, the counter must equal cap, not cap-1.
Inside the capping branch, the stored counter value must equal cap exactly.

spec.expr: cap__1 is None or counters.get(key__1, DEFAULT_INITIAL_COUNT) + 1 <= cap__1 or counters.get(key__1, DEFAULT_INITIAL_COUNT) + 1 - 1 == cap__1 - 1

constraints.intent: Verify that when cap is applied, the resulting value would be cap not cap-1.
The returned counter value must never exceed the cap.
